secondary_archetype: Technical PM focused on internal platforms and AI-enabled developer/product workflows

level_signals: Base pay range $136,000–$204,000 USD consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience working on internal platforms, developer tools, operational systems, or AI-enabled workflow products.
must_haves.qualifier: internal platforms, developer tools, operational systems, or AI-enabled workflow products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You deeply understand the product development lifecycle and have experience building systems that support planning, development, experimentation, release management, and measurement workflows.
You are excited about applying AI to improve how teams work and have experience evaluating or implementing AI-driven tooling, automation, copilots, or workflow intelligence.
You are highly collaborative and able to align stakeholders across Product, Engineering, Infrastructure, Data, and GTM teams.
You use data and operational metrics to evaluate product outcomes and identify opportunities for optimization and automation.
You bring a systems-thinking mindset and understand how workflows, tools, data, and teams connect across an organization.
You are comfortable operating in ambiguity and helping organizations define how AI should responsibly and effectively augment internal workflows.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working in high-growth SaaS environments
Experience building or integrating AI copilots, workflow automation, or internal AI tooling
Familiarity with LLM-powered products, prompt workflows, or AI orchestration systems
Experience with developer productivity or developer experience platforms
Background in engineering, technical product management, data platforms, or developer tooling
Experience driving AI adoption and change management within product or engineering organizations

explicitly_excluded: Fully remote candidates will not be considered at this time

scope.surface: Internal AI-enabled platforms supporting the product development lifecycle (planning, development, launch, measurement) across Product and Engineering teams
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS (ecommerce marketing platform); this role is internal-facing — internal teams treated as customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Reduction in operational friction and manual work
Speed of product development lifecycle (plan → build → launch → measure)
core_responsibilities: Define and own the product strategy for AI-enabled internal platforms that support the full product development lifecycle.
Reimagine how teams move from product planning → development → launch → measurement through intelligent workflows, AI copilots, and integrated tooling.
Identify friction points, repetitive manual work, and operational inefficiencies, then deliver AI-driven solutions that improve productivity and decision-making.
Partner with engineering, infrastructure, and data teams to build scalable internal systems enhanced with automation, generative AI, and workflow intelligence.

Introduce AI-powered capabilities such as automated project and launch coordination, AI-generated summaries, documentation, and status updates, and AI-assisted experimentation analysis and rollout recommendations.
Help define how AI is embedded into internal PM and operational workflows across the organization.
Drive adoption of AI-enabled tooling and establish best practices for AI-assisted product development workflows.
Serve as a thought partner on the future of AI-enabled product development operations and internal developer productivity.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear product surface: internal AI-enabled platforms, not vague customer-facing scope
Explicit encouragement to apply even for close-but-not-exact matches
Well-defined responsibilities and structured 'Nice to Have' section
AI fluency expectations clearly articulated (responsible use, bias awareness, human-in-the-loop)
red_flags: Strictly hybrid: 3 days/week in Boston office — fully remote candidates explicitly excluded
High buzzword density around 'AI' may indicate early-stage or aspirational internal platform rather than mature product
Role title on posting ('Senior Product Manager, Product Experience') differs from role descriptor in body ('Senior PM, Product Experience & AI Enablement') — minor inconsistency
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international applicants

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior PM' and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — ecommerce SaaS; role itself is internal-facing)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from statement 'reflects the cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets')
ambiguities: Role title in identity fields is 'Senior Product Manager, Product Experience' but JD body refers to 'Senior PM, Product Experience & AI Enablement' — treated as the same role.
Equity is mentioned as part of the total compensation package but no specific form (RSUs, options) is described.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned in the JD body.
